issues.title: Effort allocation + research→papers→conferences loop (Fable workshop #2 / mechanics review)

labels.name: strategic
labels.description: Strategic planning and high-level architectural decisions

issues.body:
Playtest fd60eb6 — 'the game feels a little weird; needs a thorough mechanics + timing check.' Core design threads for workshop #2:

1. **Effort allocation is the game's core but low-fidelity.** AP is a single global pool spent instantly; 'banking' AP exists but is unused/ineffective. The game has always been *about* allocating effort — deepen it: per-researcher activities, meaningful AP banking, researchers able to do more than one thing.
2. **Safety papers have little payoff.** If effort pyramids researchers→research→papers, papers should reward meaningfully. Right now publishing feels flat.
3. **Papers / conferences / travel are not linked.** No clear in-game process connecting them. Travel & conferences should be available EARLY — as another money sink AND a second thing for researchers/effort to go into — closing a research→papers→conferences→reputation loop.

Direction: turn effort allocation + the paper/conference loop into a source of *interesting decisions* (the richness/tension the mechanics initiative targeted). Needs a thorough mechanics-and-timing pass with Fable.

issues.title: Audit strategic options/events/buttons that cost but have no meaningful game impact
issues.body: Playtest fd60eb6: 'some of the strategic options might have events or buttons that incur costs but don't have meaningful impacts on the game.' Audit actions/events for cost-without-consequence (no-ops or negligible effects) — every cost should buy a real state change (ties to the meaningful-decisions design).

issues.title: Test suite: 14 order-dependent failures in full local run (singleton leakage), invisible to CI

labels.name: testing
labels.description: Issues related to test suite and testing infrastructure

issues.body: Surfaced by #582/#589: once the stale-load-error mask was removed, a FULL local GUT run (`python scripts/run_godot_tests.py`) shows ~14 failures in test_game_manager.gd (13) + test_turn_manager.gd (1) — difficulty money (Easy asserts 367500, gets 225000), a $25k salary deduction, and signal/turn-flow timing. **CI's Unit Tests gate passes** (main + #589 green), so these are order-dependent, not caught in CI's mode. Almost certainly singleton/state leakage between tests (GameConfig.difficulty etc.) rather than game bugs — difficulty modifiers verifiably work in-game (#585). Fix: proper per-test isolation (reset GameConfig/singletons in before_each/after_each); then confirm whether any are genuine regressions vs pure test artifacts.

issues.title: Playtest design ideas: payroll depth, donor base, accounting-software introduces the ledger
issues.body: From live playtest, for workshop #2: (1) PAYROLL DEPTH — reviewing pay/hours is tedious *by design*; make paying payroll cost AP + occasional timesheet-chasing with disorganised scientists, so the player *wants* to automate it via an upgrade. (2) FUNDRAISING -> DONOR BASE — networking-for-rep-to-fundraise could build into a recurring donor base (ties to funding-relationship #395 + ledger receivables). (3) ACCOUNTING-SOFTWARE UPGRADE introduces/gates the Liability Ledger — an old forgotten upgrade idea; diegetically explains how the player 'gets' the ledger, and connects to 'spending buys sight' (buy accounting software = buy financial situational awareness = ledger becomes visible/manageable). Also minor: shooing the office cat gave no doom feedback.

issues.title: Balance/design from session 2 (workshop #2): events depth, upgrade rebalance, compute economy, investor-pressure frequency
issues.body: Playtest session 2, for workshop #2: (1) EVENTS — trade-offs should be more fluid than pre-committed (reference game variables), offer >2 options, enable small stacking effects. (2) UPGRADES wildly out of balance ('comfy office chairs = $120k'); the whole upgrade mechanic has lagged and needs a refocus/sophistication pass (ties office cosmetics #470/#471). (3) COMPUTE ECONOMY — researchers request/burn compute at different rates. (4) Investor-pressure fired turns 10 AND 12 — event pool too small / frequency off; expand pool or check the mechanic.

issues.title: Legibility pass (buildable soon): doom blow-by-blow, ledger/overlay discoverability, researcher info, event fanfare
issues.body: Playtest session 2, high-value legibility ideas: (1) DOOM BLOW-BY-BLOW — verbal + colour-coded per-component doom changes in the enhanced doom view ('rivals +4, momentum +1'), tied to SA; the doom_sources breakdown already exists in state. (2) LEDGER/DEV-SCREEN DISCOVERABILITY — not obvious where the ledger is; add visible access + floating reminders for dev screens/overlays during beta. (3) RESEARCHER INFO on hire screen via hover/select using existing profile data (traits/skill/loyalty/salary). (4) EVENT FANFARE — Civ-style fade-up popup w/ simple graphic + flavor for momentous events/unlocks instead of a button silently appearing.

issues.title: UI jitter on hover/button; layout realignment when new actions (Strategic moves) appear
issues.body: Playtest session 2: minor jitters on mouseover/buttoning, possibly when Strategic moves unlocked. Also: a new action button just *appearing* causes weird realignments — see the event-fanfare design idea for a better reveal pattern.

issues.title: Keyboard input routing: hire-screen number keys dead + bug-report text field intercepted by global hotkeys
issues.body: Playtest session 2: (1) shortcut '1' opens the hire screen but then 1/2/3 don't select researchers to hire. (2) Filing a bug report (F8) is nearly impossible — typing 'n','t' etc. triggers global hotkey popups instead of entering text. Both are input-focus/routing bugs: sub-screen number keys not wired, and text fields not capturing input away from global shortcuts. (Backend submit was tested and works.)

issues.title: Event dialog keyboard: choice keys mismatch shown buttons ('key index out of range')
issues.body: Playtest #560 log: pressing 'r' in an event dialog logged 'Key index 3 out of range or not found'. Event choice keybinds don't map to the displayed buttons. Fix dialog key mapping.

issues.title: Ledger loan billing zeroes money AND crashes reputation; magnitudes don't reconcile
issues.body: Playtest #560 turn-5->6 log: a loan billing took money 345k->0 AND reputation 50->7.5 in one turn (near reputation<=0 instant-loss), while death_attribution named only ~21k. Investigate billing magnitude + reputation damage. Also: loan terms not surfaced; add 'pay the bill' interaction (UX, may split).

issues.title: Travel/Conferences only reachable by hotkey T, absent from menus
issues.body: Playtest #560: Travel/Conferences don't appear in the left menu or submenus; only the T hotkey. Add a menu affordance.

issues.title: Cross-repo sync: careful cleanup pass (pdoom1 -> pdoom1-website / pdoom-data)
issues.body:
Deferred from the CI Tier-3 sweep (PR #543, decision E). This touches **secrets and live pushes to sibling repos**, so it needs a careful, tested pass rather than riding along with routine CI cleanup.

## How repo-syncing works today (context)

`pdoom1` is the **source of truth**; content flows *outward* to two siblings (per `docs/sync-config.yml`, `conflict_resolution: source_wins`):

- **`PipFoweraker/pdoom1-website`** — receives docs, dev-blog, game-version/release data.
- **`PipFoweraker/pdoom-data`** — receives shared docs; *should* receive release notifications (but doesn't — see below).

Two workflows actually push live today:
- `sync-documentation.yml` — on push to `main` touching `docs/shared/**` etc., pushes into both siblings' `docs/` via `secrets.CROSS_REPO_TOKEN`.
- `sync-dev-blog.yml` — on push touching `dev-blog/entries/**`, pushes into the website's `public/blog/` via `secrets.WEBSITE_SYNC_TOKEN`.

Plus one *pull*-based path: `docs-sync.yml` builds `_website_export/` and uploads it as an artifact the website is expected to pull.

## What this pass should fix

1. **Dead path mappings** in `docs/sync-config.yml` / `sync-documentation.yml`: `docs/website/`, `docs/templates/`, `API_REFERENCE.md`, `DATABASE_SCHEMA.md` map from paths that don't exist -> silent no-ops. Remove or point at real sources. (Only `docs/shared/*` actually flows today.)
2. **Domain drift**: sync-bot commit emails use `docs-sync+bot@pdoom.net` / `dev+blog-sync@pdoom.net`. Canonical domain is now **`pdoom1.com`** (confirmed 2026-06). Normalise.
3. **pdoom-data release-notify**: in `sync-game-version.yml` the `repository_dispatch` to pdoom-data (`event_type: game_version_update`) is **commented out** -> pdoom-data is never told about releases. Decide: implement or remove.
4. **Two overlapping PATs**: `CROSS_REPO_TOKEN` (docs + version sync) and `WEBSITE_SYNC_TOKEN` (dev-blog) both write to `pdoom1-website`. Consider consolidating to one.
5. **Verify the two live paths still work** end-to-end after edits (test on a throwaway change), since a mistake here silently breaks propagation to public surfaces.

## Risk / approach

- Editing these files can break live sibling-repo pushes without an obvious failure signal. Do it as its own PR, test with a trivial `docs/shared/` change, and confirm the sibling repos receive it before merging.
- Related dev-note: capture "how the ecosystem syncs" in the CI-strategy dev-notes (PR #543 decision B) so this isn't re-discovered later.

_Filed from the documentation/CI consistency audit._

issues.title: ci: bump deprecated Node-20 GitHub Actions (checkout/setup-python/setup-node/upload-artifact)
issues.body:
## Problem

GitHub Actions runs are emitting deprecation annotations because several workflows
pin action versions whose runtime targets **Node.js 20**, which GitHub is now
force-running on Node.js 24 and will eventually stop supporting:

> Node.js 20 is deprecated. The following actions target Node.js 20 but are being
> forced to run on Node.js 24 ...
> (https://github.blog/changelog/2025-09-19-deprecation-of-node-20-on-github-actions-runners/)

Surfaced while repairing the two startup-broken workflows in #530. Not urgent (the
runner currently auto-forces Node 24), but should be patched before GitHub drops
the Node 20 fallback.

## Affected (at least)

Seen in annotations on `data-validation.yml` and `dev-blog-automation.yml`:
- `actions/checkout@v4`
- `actions/setup-python@v4`
- `actions/setup-node@v4`
- `actions/upload-artifact@v4`

Note `data-validation.yml` already uses `actions/setup-python@v5` in some jobs but
`@v4` in others — inconsistent, worth normalizing.

## Suggested fix

Sweep **all** `.github/workflows/*.yml` and bump deprecated actions to a
Node-24-based major:
- `actions/checkout@v4` → `@v5`
- `actions/setup-python@v4` → `@v5`
- `actions/setup-node@v4` → `@v5`
- `actions/upload-artifact@v4` → confirm latest (v4 is the current major; check
  whether a newer Node-24 patch/major exists at patch time)

Pin to the latest stable major for each, verify each workflow still parses
(`python -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open(f))"`) and check a dispatch run shows
no remaining Node 20 deprecation annotations.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] No `Node.js 20 is deprecated` annotations on any workflow run
- [ ] Action versions consistent across all workflows
- [ ] Workflows still start and run (no new startup failures)

issues.title: Insight System (Skill-Gated Visibility)
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement an insight system that gates player visibility into game mechanics based on character skills.

**Related to**: Risk System design (see `godot/docs/design/RISK_SYSTEM.md`)

---

## Feature Description

Player insight into risk, consequences, and NPC reactions scales with **Insight** skills. Inspired by Civilization's diplomacy system where higher relationship/intel reveals more about AI intentions.

### Insight Levels

| Level | What Player Sees |
|-------|------------------|
| **None (0)** | Outcomes only, no explanations |
| **Low (1-2)** | Vague hints: "Reaction will likely be mixed" |
| **Medium (3-4)** | Directional: "This will increase regulatory scrutiny" |
| **High (5-6)** | Specific: "OpenPhil dislikes this; Anthropic neutral" |
| **Expert (7+)** | Quantified: "Regulatory Attention +8, Financial Exposure +3" |

### Insight Domains

Different skills reveal different aspects:
- **Political Insight** → Regulatory Attention, Public Awareness visibility
- **Financial Insight** → Financial Exposure visibility  
- **Academic Insight** → Research Integrity, Capability Overhang visibility
- **Organizational Insight** → Insider Threat visibility

### Acquisition

Insight can be developed through:
- Character creation (allocate points at game start)
- Upgrades and investments during play
- Experience in specific domains (repeated interactions)

---

## Design Notes

From design discussion (2025-12-25):
> "Players can have relative insight into the precision of the impacts of their plays by gaining skill in those kinds of interactions over time... player's Insight level vary from hints 'reaction to this will likely be mixed' to specific 'OpenPhil will seriously dislike this, Anthropic will be neutral' similar to advanced diplomacy giving you more insight into things in the game of Civilization."

Key principle: Some players prefer operating with limited information (challenge), others want full visibility (strategy optimization). The system accommodates both playstyles.

---

## Implementation Notes

### Phase 1 (Current - Risk System)
- Dev mode toggle: full visibility on/off
- No skill gating yet

### Phase 2 (This Issue)
- Integrate with character creation
- Implement skill-based visibility filtering
- Add upgrade paths for insight skills

---

## Integration Points

- **Risk System**: Insight determines how much risk pool info is shown
- **Character Creation**: Insight skills allocated at game start
- **Events**: Causal explanations gated by insight level
- **Actions**: Pre-action feedback ("This will likely...") gated by insight
- **NPCs/Funders**: Relationship visibility gated by political insight

---

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Insight levels affect displayed information
- [ ] At least 4 insight domains implemented
- [ ] Character creation integration
- [ ] Upgrade path for insight skills
- [ ] Graceful degradation (low insight still playable)
- [ ] Dev mode bypasses insight gating

issues.title: Revisit terminal/CRT texture aesthetic (parked art package)
issues.body:
## What this is
A never-merged **visual direction** for the game UI: a retro **terminal / CRT** aesthetic plus a library of **institutional surface textures**, with `theme_manager` integration and several menus already retextured. Parked during the June 2026 branch cleanup so it isn't lost — to be revisited when we next work on UI/UX.

## Why parked, not merged
It's an art-direction call, not a bug/feature — worth a deliberate "do we want this look?" decision while actively doing UI work, rather than merging blind or dropping.

## What's in the package (~20 PNG textures + generation prompts)
- `godot/assets/textures/surfaces/` — bakelite (cracked), concrete (institutional), CRT burn-in, grid (circuit-trace / aged graphpaper / perforated metal), linoleum (damaged), oxidized copper, painted metal panel, plywood (stained)
- `godot/assets/textures/terminal/` — amber (noise/scanlines), blue (BSOD/DOS bg), cyan (border), green (grid/scanlines), etc.
- `art_prompts/batch_4_terminal_textures.yaml` — the prompts used to generate them (so the set is reproducible/extendable)
- `godot/autoload/theme_manager.gd` — integration hooks
- Retextured scenes: `welcome`, `settings_menu`, `pregame_setup`, `leaderboard_screen`, `end_game_screen`
- `godot/scripts/ui/doom_meter.gd` — minor texture tweak

## How to recover / preview
- Permanent tag (survives branch cleanup): `git checkout parked/ui-crt-textures`
- Original branch tip: `feature/ui-texture-integration` @ `5c0b74a` (3 commits, +1200 lines)
- To see it live: rebase the tag onto current `main` and run in Godot.

## When picking this up
- Decide if the CRT/terminal look fits the current UI direction.
- Even if not adopting wholesale, the texture set + `art_prompts` yaml are reusable — cherry-pick individual surfaces/overlays.

_Parked from `feature/ui-texture-integration`; assets preserved at tag `parked/ui-crt-textures`._
